HANK'S BEDROOM - NIGHT

HANK GROTOWSKI wakes from his dream. Stares at the room. Sweating. Breathing hard. Jumps out of bed and bolts for the bathroom. SOUND of Hank throwing up MILITARY CARBINE, M-16, mounted above an ITHACA .22. PHOTOS ON DRESSER:
HANK (age 10) with his father BUCK (age 30) --
Dressed in hunting garb. Standing over a fallen deer, rifles in hand.
Hank wears a Marine uniform. Buck stands next to him, a
Both wear correction officer uniforms.
HANK ( 3 0 ' s ) , BUCK ( 5 0 ' s ) , and Hank's son, SONNY (10) --
All three dressed in hunting garb. They stand over a fallen deer, rifles in hand. Proud hunters.
HANK (40' s ) , BUCK (60's), SONNY (20's) -- Three generations in correction officer uniforms. Hank and
Buck are grim. Sonny flashes a sardonic smile.
BATHROOM - NIGHT
Hank splashes water on his face. Hank wipes his mouth with a towel. He stares into his eyes for an answer. Takes a finger and traces the wrinkles on his tired face.
Rev. Blue 4/30/01
Hank is stocky and muscular. 46 years old. A boyish quality about him.
HALLWAY - NIGHT
Hank staggers out of bathroom, holding his stomach. Stops in front of . Hank gently turns knob. Locked. Hank moves on.
HANK'S BEDROOM - NIGHT
Hank gets dressed.
ESTABLISH HANK'S HOME - NIGHT
Split--level brick house sits on two acres of land. Lawn needs cutting. Garage at end of driveway. Quiet road. Crickets can be heard.
BACK YARD
A barb-wire fence marks the property's boundary.( Just inside the perimeter. Their faces covered by weeds.
HANK'S HOME - NIGHT
Hank walks down from back porch. Gets in WHITE CADILLAC parked in driveway. Engine starts, Cadillac pulls out onto road.
ESTABLISH WAFFLE HOUSE DINER - NIGHT A down home greasy spoon. Local franchise.
White Cadillac pulls into lot.
WAFFLE HOUSE DINER - NIGHT
Hank walks in. LUCILLE, the waitress, greets him at the entrance.
Evening, Cowboy. Lucille. Hank walks to his usual booth. Sits down. WAITRESS (BETTY). A college student. Walks by Hank as she delivers a food order.
Evening.
Lucille brings a bowl of chocolate ice and a cup of coffee. Places them in front of Hank. Sits across from him.
Thank you.
How's Sonny? Fine.
LOCUST GROVE, MAIN STREET - SAME NIGHT
Busy street. Cars honking. Cable TV. Vibrating Beds.
Rooms by the Hour. MOTEL ROOM - NIGHT

A cheap BED. Tattered WALLS. STAINS of all shapes, sizes and color on the CARPET. FORMICA END TABLE has been used as an ashtray. Opened PINT OF WILD TURKEY and CAN OF COKE on end table. cigarette burns in the ASHTRAY. SONNY GROTOWSKI sits on the bed in his underpants, sipping from a paper cup. 24 years old. Taller than his father. Lean and muscular. Not happy with who he is. He gets up and goes to the
BATHROOM -NIGHT ,
Sonny looks at himself in the bathroom mirror. A KNOCK at the DOOR. Sonny steps out of bathroom and opens door. VERA. Early 30' s . looking pro. Heavy makeup. Tight dress.
Haggard-
She enters. Sonny closes door.
Doing okay, Vera. How you been?
He hands her cash.
Fine, thanks.
She drops the money in her purse without counting it. A drink?
Thank you, Hon.
Grabs pint and kicks back a slug of whiskey. She pulls off her dress. Sonny finishes his drink. She steps out of her panties. He takes off his unde.rpants.
I ' m sore, so go easy, Darling. Yes Ma'am.
..He takes her from the rear and starts pumping her against the table. Sonny quickly cums and falls on chair. Vera gets up and starts dressing.
Thanks, Vera.
You're welcome.
He warches her pull on the tight dress. Vera catches Sonny staring at her.
What's the master, Hon? You look so sad.
I' m fine. Hey, you wanna get: a bite to eat? Talk?
She rubs his hair and pats his shoulder.
(laughing) You take care, Sonny.
She goes out the door. Sonny sits up on the bed. Scares at the ceiling. Spits on the floor. Lays back.
HANK'S DRIVEWAY - MORNING
Cadillac stops at end of driveway. pulls newspaper from mailbox. Hank reaches out and Hank tightly rolls up the paper as he walks up front porch.
BUCK'S ROOM - DAY

Hank enters and drops newspaper on table. Picks up coffee pot. Starts filling a cup. It's cold. BUCK enters from side porch. Carries a large scrap book. Buck is 74 years old. Paying the price of emphysema. He steadies himself on a walker which has an oxygen tank strapped to it. A green tube runs to his nose. He struggles to table. (without looking up)
Hank exits with coffee pot and cup. Buck looks at front page of paper. LAWRENCE MUSGROVE DIES TONIGHT Buck proceeds to cut out the article and place it in his book.
BUCK'S ROOM - DAY

Hank and Buck at the table drinking coffee and eating sausage and eggs. Cold silence dominates the room. ,i Buck looks out the window and notices TWO BLACK BOYS walking across the back yard, heading towards the driveway. What the hell those niggers doing out
Hank cranes his neck to look out the window. Goes back to eating.
I said something to you. Hear me?
Hank takes a sip of coffee. Damn porch monkeys, be moving in here soon." Sirring next to me, watching my TV. Was a time when they knew they're place, wasn't none of this mixing going on.
Hank gets up. Pulls a 12-GAUGE SHOTGUN from the hallway. Grabs box of shells off the closet shelf. Watches Hank. Takes another bite of sausage.
YARD - DAY

The BOYS walk close together. Brothers WILLIE, 14, and DARRYL, 11 years old. Good kids. Sonny's Nova turns into the driveway. The boys see the car. Hank approaching. The Nova slows to a stop. Sonny leans out the window. The * boys step closer smiling. Instantly'all smiles stops. Sonny * turns sees Hank approaching. Buck watches^ from window.
The boys are scared.
Put that thing down.
We came by ro see Sonny. Hank raises Shotgun towards the sky and pulls the trigger. The gun lets out a DEAFENING BLAST. away. The kids turn and sprint
You're a real hardass, scaring those boys. I want them off my property.
Father and son exchange hateful looks. Sonny peels off. Buck still watching from window.
BUCK'S DEN - LATER THAT DAY
Hank in uniform. A pistol at his waist. from the kitchen counter. Buck sits watching TV. book on his lap. Picks up his keys CONFEDERATE FLAG next to TV.
Got everything you need? Yup.
Watch yourself walking around . . . Yeah, yeah.
Hank leaves. Buck lets the scrap book fall flat to the floor. foot, gently slides it under the recliner. With his
HANK'S DRIVEWAY - A FEW MOMENTS LATER

Hank comes out the kitchen door and heads to Cadillac. the foot of the drive he sees Willie, Darryl, and their father, RYRUS COOPER. A tall man. Even tempered. Hank gets in Cadillac. Keeps his eyes on Ryrus. The Caddy heads to the foot of drive. Ryrus approaches Hank's window. Willie and Darryl stand about 15 feet back. Watching their father. Hank rolls his window down.
See these two boys here?
Ryrus points to Darryl and Willie.
They my sons. You hear?
Hank's hand wraps around his revolver.
They friends of your boy.
THROUGH A VIDEO CAMERA LENS, BLACK & WHITE, TIME-CODE FLASHING:
DEATH CHAMBER - LATE DAY
A MASKED FACE All strapped down.
The BODY convulses. STRAPS. Pushing forward against the LEATHER An ARM works- itself free. VOICES come into play.

VIDEO CAMERA TURNS OFF. WE SEE THE DEATH TEAM: HANK, SONNY, and: PHIL HUGGINS, black, big man, about 6-2, 240 Ibs. HARVEY SHOONMAKER, white, a big guy himself, almost as big as Phil, a lot fatter. TOMMY ROULAINE, tall, lean white man in his early 30 ' s , crew cut. GEORGIA ANN PAYNES, early 30s, only woman on the team. They surround the ELECTRIC CHAIR and undo the buckles and mask, freeing from the chair's grip: DAPPA SMITH, a wiry black man in his 40s, the last member of the DEATH TEAM.
I'm okay
All eyes go to the ARM'S STRAP.
Sonny goes a bit stiff.
It was my mistake. Won't happen next time.
Hank looks at Sonny. Hank looks at his TEAM.
Georgia Ann stands in the corner of the room, operating a handheld video camera.
(to Georgia Ann) Camera ready?
Georgia Ann puts camera to eye. RED LIGHT goes on. Camera ready. Tape rolling. THROUGH VIDEO CAMERA LENS, BLACK & WHITE, TIME-CODE FLASHING: Let's keep going until we get it
Hank steals a look to Georgia Ann and Sonny.
And ladies.
PRISON - DUSK Atmospheric shots around penitentiary. BAR AND GRILL - DUSK The place is quiet tonight.

Hank, Phil, Harvey, Dappa, Georgia Ann and Tommy sit around the table. PITCHER OF BEER in middle of table. Plates of food and glasses of beer in front of everybody except Tommy who drinks water. They eat and drink as they talk.
Hank exhibits an easiness among his team buddies.
That's none of your business. Whooooaaa!
You know, it really offends me to hear you talking that away, especially being a woman and all.
Better watch it, Annie, Tommy's gonna sue you for sexual harassment. Georgia Ann snuggles up to Tommy.
We can settle outta court.
Everyone laughs.
(smiles) Fine, we'll settle this in church. They.laugh harder.
Hank glances at
BAR AREA
BOOTER (barmaid), hands pack of cigarettes to Sonny from front of bar. She's a pretty girl.
Fairly drunk. Leans into her.
Booter, how you doing? Just gimme the two fifty, Sonny.
Sonny digs into his pockets when
slaps money onto the bar.
Hank pulls Sonny to a chair. They sit alone.
No more mistakes. I want to be absolutely sure that nobody makes a mistake, especially you.
Sonny starts packing his cigarettes.
When the time comes for us, you, me, the other guys, to do it, we got to do it proper. You got that?
(fake smile) Yes Sir.
Cause I do not, I repeat, DO NOT want to have to go out there and have to say, "Fuck, well, you know, my son said he understood the process, but, well, he just made a mistake."
Sonny nods. Hank leans in.

In England, they used to give the condemned man a party the night before . . . called it the Monster's Ball . . . we owe it to him. He don't want no preacher or lawyer with him. So it's just gonna be me and you. I don't care what he done, we got to make the last hours easy as possible for him.
Hank leans back and studies his son's face.
I'd appreciate you saying something.
I gotta take a piss.
Sonny gets up. Walks away.
POND - DAY DUCKS AND GEESE
Some paddle across the water. Others walk around pond's edge, picking at grass. In the background OLD GRAY DODGE DART moves across horizon. floats from the back. Blue exhaust

BLACK WOMAN'S HANDS Hold steering wheel. Gold wedding band on left ring finger. Temperature gauge needle in red zone.
ROAD - DAY
Dart passes sign at side of road:
PARKING LOT - DAY
Dart pulls in.
Parks in space near

A white stone building. CORRECTION OFFICER stands at gate. The black woman, LETICIA MUSGROVE, and her son, TYRELL, get out and start running toward building. Leticia is African American, close to 40. Her beautiful face worn and exhausted. A dignity about her. Tyrell is an 11 year old boy. Suffers from obesity. Round face. Expectant eyes. Would do anything for his mother.
Tyrell can't keep up with Leticia.
Come on! Move itI
INT. DEATH ROW ENTRANCE - DAY
Leticia and Tyrell pass security cameras.
DEATH ROW VISITING ROOM - DAY

* A narrow room. Two long benches. Feet dangling from the bench. Sits opposite his son. Smoking a cigarette. Lawrence is lean, about 45 years old. Dark-skinned African-American with salt n pepper hair. An
Three white, one black. Very big men. Arms crossed. Keeping close watch. Stands against the far wall. Fumbles through her purse for a cigarette. LETICIA'S HANDS Tremble as she lights up.
Yeah.
What you drawing?
Things, stuff I see.
Like what?
Guys getting killed. people.
TV shows, ya know,
That's funny. I've been drawing what I see too^ Ain't a whole lot to see in here, but what there is, I draw.
I'm not too good at drawing animals.
Go to a zoo, study the monkeys. You can draw a monkey, you can draw anything.
Tyrell smiles. Lets out a laugh. Lawrence smiles. ' Lawrence loses control. interrupts.
Gasps for breath.
Lawrence pulls himself together. Laughs it off. CO with a wave of his hand. Dismisses The CO smiles at Tyrell and fades back. Who's he?
Just some guy, a guard. We don't have a lot of time. I wanna give you all my drawings and my clothes . . . won't fit you now, but when you get older, you might want them.
I ain't gonna see you again after
Cause I'm a bad man. Who says?
I do.
Tyrell shakes his head.
You ain' t me. Yes I am.

No! You are not. (beat) You are the best of what I am, that's what you are. But the man you see here right now, you are not. Leticia interrupts her husband and son. Hey, Baby. You look nice today. You
always look nice. Leticia keeps cool.
(to Tyrell) You tell your father what you won at school?
CONTINUED: ( 3 )
Oh, yeah, right.
Lawrence's face lights up,
What's that?
My drawing is getting used on the school magazine cover. Know what I did?
Leticia makes to hit Tyrell. Holds back.
Tyrell, don't play no guessing games. Just tell him.
I made a picture of you. Sitting alone. In jail.
Lawrence is puzzled.
The theme was solitude.
Damn, Boy, put it there.
Puts out his hand. Tyrell shows him the latest school handshake. Lawrence laughs.
Not too bad, Ty. Not too bad. (beat.) (to Leticia) The car?
It leaks radiator fluid.
Take it in right away. Have 'em check the hoses and stuff. You let that car run too hot, you're in trouble.
They look at each other.
The house?
I' m losing it. I can't make the payments. Not no more.
Tyrell looks around. Sees a window covered with wire mesh. Goes to it and stares out.* Lawrence takes Leticia's hand. She gently pulls away.
Look, I want you to say goodbye to
Lawrence. Resigned.
Been coming here for damn near eleven years. It's like my home" now, and I don't . . .
A white CO#1 interrupts. Time.
So soon?
White CO gives Lawrence a knowing look.
Well, I gotta get going, baby. Tyrell walks up to Lawrence. Lawrence pats Tyrell on the shoulder. Tyrell grabs his father's leg.
The COs start to make for Tyrell. Lawrence puts up a hand.
Lawrence puts his hand on Leticia's shoulder. Whispers in her ear.
Tyrell, just let me go, and I promise
For every time I hurt you, I'm sorry. Leticia watches two white C0s#l&2 take Lawrence away. Then Collapses into a chair. Breaks down. Remaining white CO#3 takes Leticia's hand and helps her back up.
Leticia stands. Stifles herself.
Come on. Let's go.
Tyrell takes his mother's hand. They turn and leave. White CO#3 starts to usher her out by the elbow. Leticia turns and smacks him across the face.
Don't touch mei
Face goes red. Checks his temper.
I'm not your prisoner, SIR! We know
the way out. Black CO puts his hand on Leticia's shoulder.
Don't you say nothing to me.
DEATH ROW CELL - DAY

Lawrence cleans out his.cell. Meticulously stacks a series of black & white drawings. Each depicts various aspects of life on death row: GUARDS, PRISONERS, INDIVIDUALS AND GROUPS, CELLS, CORRIDORS, THE ELECTRIC CHAIR. Lawrence places them on top of a piece of cardboard. He puts another piece of cardboard on top of the drawings. Carefully tapes the corners. Lawrence places drawings and the rest of his meager belongings into a large cardboard box. .....* LAWRENCE'S HANDS Tape the box. He places a stick--on address label on top which reads:
34 Dalonaga Road Locust Grove, GA 33104
Now stands in an empty cell holding all his belongings. He hands the box to a CORRECTION OFFICER.
Careful with this, okay? Please? Don't worry. Worry is all I got.
SPECIAL ESCORT TEAM LOCKER ROOM - DAY
THE SPECIAL ESCORT TEAM members are getting into their uniforms. Quiet. .Psyching themselves.
Hank grabs Sonny's arm.
Sonny yanks free.
Get your hands off me.
Hank walks away. A large BLACK HAND lands on Sonny's shoulder. Sonny turns to see Phil.
No.
Phil lets out a knowing grin.
Everyone's nervous first time out. Remember, we're all here for you.
Phil looks around the room.
We're family. We'll help get you
through this.
I'll be alright.
Phil gives him a little hug.
I'll be fine.
SPECIAL ESCORT TEAM CONFERENCE ROOM - DAY
All the COs, in uniform, are sitting at a long table. Hank leads the group.
He doesn't have any outstanding appeals.
All right. Everybody feeling okay?
The SPECIAL ESCORT TEAM nods "yes."
Anybody got anything to say?
A HAND goes up.
He likes to draw. Calms him down. Thanks.
The SPECIAL ESCORT TEAM gets up from their seats and forms a circle, holding hands.
Our Father, who art in Heaven . . .
ESTABLISH LETICIA'S HOUSE - DAY Neighborhood of "shotgun houses." Worn, dilapidated homes.
Dirt yards. Unpaved street. Leticia' s Dodge Dart parked in yard.
LETICIA'S HOME - DAY
Used, worn furniture. Tiny, but clean. Leticia and Tyrell sit on beat-up couch, watching TV. She smokes a cigarette. TELEPHONE between them.
INT. LAWRENCE'S CELL - DAY
Hank and Sonny enter. Sonny carries SHACKLE BELT.
(approaching Lawrence) I ' m going to put these on nice and loose. Everything's going to be OK, right? That's right.
Sonny shackles Lawrence as Hank watches.
My last phone call? Warden says it's a bad idea. I understand. You think maybe he could call my son, tell him that I tried. I don't think so.
Lawrence lets his head drop. Stares at floor.
LETICIA'S HOME - DAY
Leticia stubs out cigarette. Gets up from couch. Goes to dining room table. Picks up purse. Digs through it, picking out coins and placing them on the table. She scoops up change. Turns to Tyrell.
I'll be right back.
Listens to door open and slam shut. He jumps up from couch and goes into bedroom.
DEATH ROW - CORRIDOR - DAY
Hank and Sonny escort Lawrence to DEATH CELL. Shackle chains rattle against his legs.
DEATH CELL - NIGHT A toilet, a shower stall, and a table bolted to the floor in

the center of the cell. There is a bench on each long side of the table; they are also bolted to the floor. On the table is a metal ashtray, bolted to the table, and a HOLY BIBLE. This is where Lawrence will spend his last three hours. Hank, Sonny and Lawrence enter the death cell. Sonny undoes Lawrence's ankles, then his wrists, as Lawrence looks around the cell.
Hank puts his hand up. His manner firm and soothing.
Your paper and pencil are on the way. They're on the way, I promise.

Sonny tosses the shackles to Hank. on a hook at the cell's entrance. Hank hangs the shackles Lawrence pulls a pack of cigarettes from his shirt pocket. He pulls one out and places it in his mouth. Sonny immediately whips out a Zippo and lights Lawrence's cigarette.
You're welcome.
DEATH CELL - SHORT WHILE LATER - NIGHT
Sits at the table drawing.

Sits across from Lawrence. Very still. Posing. Sits in a chair by the entrance. Arms folded. Alert. Lawrence sings the 70's tune "Will It Go Round in Circles?," as he draws. Image taking shape. Four butts lie in the tray. Falls from Lawrence's hand and hits the floor. Lawrence wipes his brow and picks up pencil. Takes out another cigarette. Sonny again immediately lights Lawrence's cigarette. back to pose.
You're welcome.
Lawrence takes a drag from cigarette. Continues to draw.
LETICIA'S HOME - SAME NIGHT

Tyrell sits on couch, eating a large candy bar. Telephone on his lap. DOOR The LOCK turns. Leticia enters, carrying brown bag. Tyrell quickly hides the candy bar under cushion. Leticia sits next to Tyrell. She removes a tall beer from bag and pops it open. Takes a large^hit.
DEATH CELL - SAME NIGHT

Dappa enters with LAWRENCE'S LAST MEAL. A tray loaded with fried chicken, a bowl of crawfish and a piece of apple pie with vanilla ice cream on top. Hank takes tray from Dappa and places it on table. Lawrence finishes drawing of Sonny. Looks at food. Picks up DRAWING and offers it to Sonny. Sonny accepts. A very good sketch of SONNY. I don't look this good in person. Thank-you.

You're welcome. Sonny folds the drawing. Puts it in his shirt pocket. Hank gives Sonny a cold, hard look. Lawrence slides his pad and pencil away and pulls tray of food toward him. He picks .up a piece of chicken and brings it to his mouth. His hands shake as he tries to take a bite. Within seconds Lawrence's body trembles violently. He drops the chicken and puts the back of his hands to his eyes.
Sonny gives Lawrence's shoulders a soft pat. Lawrence looks deep into SONNY'S EYES. He leaps up and hugs Sonny.
Hank pulls them apart.
Sonny does. Hank gently sits Lawrence down. Hank takes deep, slow breaths as he pats Lawrence's hands. Lawrence breathes with Hank, and slowly stops shaking.

Lawrence is now drawing Hank. Sonny sits by the entrance. Something's different. Untouched food. Overflows with butts.
photograph. Takes a human being to

Tyrell stares at TV. Leticia stares at him. Feels his mother's eyes. He puts the phone down and slowly makes for his room.,
Wait a minute. Come here.
Tyrell walks back to his mother. She wipes tiny spot of chocolate off his mouth.
Look at you! What'd I tell you about
eating this shit?
Mamma, I' m sorry.

Look at you. Look at that disgusting
fat body. Tyrell falls back on sofa.
Please, let me have my shirt.
Where are they?
TYRELL'S BEDROOM - NIGHT
Tyrell pulls back his blanket to reveal a package of CANDY BARS. Leticia grabs them off the bed. This room has no order. A fat little pig lives here.
She pulls Tyrell into

Leticia forces Tyrell onto the scale. She looks at the reading. Bends his head down with her hand.
Read it out loud to me.
Leticia, with full force, gives Tyrell an uppercut smack on his face and eye. He falls onto bed and then to the floor. He starts bawling.
watches her child cry. Tyrell sobs louder and louder. Leticia gently lifts him from the floor.
Let's go wait for Daddy's call.
DEATH CELL - A SHORT WHILE LATER - SAME NIGHT

Lawrence sits on chair right pants leg being cut off. Harvey shaves Lawrence's right ankle with an electric razor. ELECTRIC CLIPPER glides over LAWRENCE'S HEAD. LAWRENCE'S HANDS sift his HAIR as it slowly falls to the cement floor. Sonny notices Lawrence's DRAWING OF HANK sitting on the table. He picks it up, folds it, places it in his shirt pocket. Harvey shaves Lawrence's.eyebrows. Harvey goes over Lawrence's fuzzy scalp with the electric razor, giving it the closest shave possible. Sonny and Hank strap a large DIAPER over Lawrence's pants. Lawrence tugs at the diaper.
A grown man wearing a diaper.

Georgia Ann hands out legal forms to the WITNESSES as they enter the room. Phil draws curtain as the witnesses enter.
DEATH CELL - NIGHT
Warden Velasco enters.
Court order in hand.
Lawrence stands, Warden reads order.
Lawrence's knees buckle. Hank helps Lawrence.steady himself.
A little tougher than I planned for.
50-A INT. HALLWAY 50-A

Sonny and Hank walk beside Lawrence down the long, dimly lit corridor. They do not touch him. LAWRENCE'S HANDS Rub along the pea--green cinder block wall. Sweat: drips down his forehead. WARDEN VELASCD, Harvey and Dappa follow . Sonny's legs give out. He collapses to the floor and vomits. Lawrence turns and reaches down to Sonny. Hank grabs Lawrence by the arm and pulls him away. Warden Velasco gently puts a hand on Hank's shoulder. The Warden 'calmly looks Lawrence in the eye, expressing a silent apology for the disruption. Warden motions for Dappa to come and take Sonny's place. Warden leans down, puts a hand on back of Sonny's neck.
Sonr.y nods "yes". The Warden helps Sonny to his feet. Dappa and Hank escort Lawrence to the' death chamber. Hank looks back as Sonny exits the hallway.

Phil and Tommy stand at each side of ELECTRIC CHAIR. curtain drawn.
The

ELECTRODE is connected to Lawrence's exposed right ankle. WARDEN VELASCO enters Checks to make sure all straps are tight and in place. Phil and Tommy draw the curtain. seated. segment-speaker: Rev. Blue 4/30/01 Lawrence Musgrove, do you have anything
segment-text: you'd like to say?

(softly)

Push the button. Warden removes microphone. A METAL CAP is placed on his shaved head. A BLACK LEATHER MASK is placed over Lawrence's face. The mask is in two parts: the lower part covers the chin and mouth; the upper part, the eyes and forehead. Only the nose is exposed. DEATH TEAM exits death chamber. Tommy Roulaine is the last to leave. He gently taps Lawrence's hand. A farewell gesture. The Warden stays. Three red buttons displayed on a console.
DEATH CHAMBER - NIGHT

Lawrence is a faceless masked man strapped in the electric chair. Warden Velasco stands in a corner. He looks at a clock which reads: LAWRENCE'S ADAMS APPLE Bobs up and down several times. Warden Velasco gives the order.
WITNESS ROOM - NIGHT
Through the glass window, the Witnesses watch Lawrence in complete silence. Lawrence's fists clench. His body convulses. A WOMAN lets out a half scream. Immediately quiets herself.
LETICIA'S BATHROOM - SAME NIGHT
Leticia is brushing her teeth. TV can be heard from the living room.
INT. SPECIAL ESCORT TEAM LOCKER ROOM BATHROOM - AN HOUR ' LATER - SAME NIGHT
Sonny bends over sink, splashing water on his face. He looks at his face in the mirror. Traces his wrinkles. Hank enters.
I said: You know what you did? You let him down. You fucked up his last walk. You made it harder for him. You fucked him up. No answer.
Hank smacks Sonny.
You are one sorry sonafabitch!
Smacks Sonny again.Sonny stands and faces his father.
What? You gonna hit me back?
Sonny slumps over and throws up. Hank steps back in disgust.
Jesus 1 Just like.a woman.
Hank hits Sonny.
Like your damn mother . . .
Sonny hits back. Fists start flying. Hank pins Sonny down and starts to hit him hard in the face. Phil arid Tommy enter the BATHROOM. They break up the fight. Phil pulls Hank off Sonny.
He's your son.
Take your hands off me nigger! Phil lets go.
Hank, this ain't you. This ain't you. Do you hear me?
You are wrong. This is me!
Let's just keep the peace. Everybody.
Hank leaves without looking at Phil. Phil puts his hand out to Sonny. Sonny refuses Phil's hand and gets up from the floor.
(to Sonny) Your mouth is bleeding pretty bad.
Sonny exits.
EXT. ESTABLISH WAFFLE HOUSE - NIGHT
Waffle House parking lot is empty.
WAFFLE HOUSE - NIGHT
Hank sits alone in a booth. Untouched plate of food in front of him. Staring. Thinking.
More coffee, handsome?
No.
You alright, Cowboy? Yeah.
She walks away. HANK Staring. Thinking.
INT. LETICIA'S HOME - EARLY MORNING
Tyrell asleep on couch. The phone rests next to his pillow. Door slams. His eyes open.
THE PINK TEA CUP - BREAKFAST TIME Southern soul food restaurant. Black neighborhood. Black
people.
"LITTLE DIXIE" CAB
pulls up. Leticia gets out. Reaches in her purse to pay driver. Change falls everywhere. Leticia frantically picks it up.
PINK TEA CUP.- BREAKFAST TIME
